title: Internet Speed Checker
description: Check your download speed instantly with a fluid stream animation that visually shows how fast your connection is. 5-round median measurement. No signup, no ads.

ui.title: Connection Speed Checker
ui.subtitle: Press the button to start measuring
ui.dataNote:
No ads · No account required
All data stays in your browser

hints.slow: OK for email, light browsing, and SD video
hints.standard: Suitable for HD video and video calls
hints.fast: Great for 4K video and large downloads
hints.ultra: Handles 4K, gaming, and multiple devices easily

howToUse.label: Press Start
howToUse.description: Tap the 'Start Test' button. The measurement begins immediately — no waiting.

howToUse.label: Wait for 5 rounds
howToUse.description: The tool fetches 500 KB of data 5 times and measures each transfer. This takes about 5–15 seconds depending on your speed.

howToUse.label: Read your result
howToUse.description: The median of 5 rounds is displayed as your final speed. The colour and animation reflect your connection speed — green/cyan is fast, red is slow.

faq.question: How accurate is this speed test?
faq.answer: The tool uses the median of 5 measurement rounds, which reduces the effect of temporary network spikes. Results are a useful guide but may vary with server load and time of day.

faq.question: How much data does one test use?
faq.answer: Each test downloads approximately 2.5 MB (500 KB × 5 rounds). Be mindful if you are on a limited mobile data plan.

faq.question: Why is my result different from other speed test sites?
faq.answer: Results vary by server location, measurement method, and the number of simultaneous connections. Use this tool as a quick reference rather than a precise benchmark.

faq.question: What is a typical home Wi-Fi speed?
faq.answer: Fibre broadband typically delivers 100–600 Mbps, while public Wi-Fi ranges from 5–50 Mbps. Mobile 4G averages around 20–50 Mbps.

faq.question: Is upload speed measured?
faq.answer: Only download speed is measured. Upload measurement requires sending data to the server, which this tool does not do.
